Intro_Warning: {M}There are violent and disturbing{N}images in this game.

ItemDesc_HealthDrink: Supplies nutrition to{N}{T}recover a small amount of{N}{T}stamina.
ItemDesc_FirstAidKit: Heals injury to provide{N}{T}moderate stamina recovery.
ItemDesc_Ampule: Relieve pain to recover{N}{T}stamina to high.{N}{T}Effect lasts for a while.
ItemDesc_LobbyKey: The key to open{N}{T}the door to the lobby.{N}{T}I found it in the locker room.
ItemDesc_HouseKey: The house key{N}{T}hidden in the dog house.
ItemDesc_KeyOfLion: The key to the garden of{N}{T}the house. Found in patrol{N}{T}car's trunk.
ItemDesc_KeyOfWoodman: The key to the garden of{N}{T}the house. Found in the{N}{T}basketball court.
ItemDesc_KeyOfScarecrow: The key to the garden of{N}{T}the house. Found in mailbox{N}{T}blocked by a fallen tree.
ItemDesc_LibraryReserveKey: The key to the school's 2nd{N}{T}floor library reserves.{N}{T}Picked up in the locker room.
ItemDesc_ClassroomKey: The tagged classroom key{N}{T}found hanging in the drainpipe{N}{T}on the roof.
ItemDesc_KGordonKey: The tag says, “K. Gordon;”{N}{T}found in the school's{N}{T}boiler room.
ItemDesc_DrawbridgeKey: Key to operate the drawbridge.{N}{T}Found in the church.
ItemDesc_BasementKey: The hospital basement{N}{T}door key, found on the table{N}{T}in 1st floor conference room.
ItemDesc_BasementStoreroomKey: The hospital basement{N}{T}storeroom key found{N}{T}in the operating room.
ItemDesc_ExaminationRoomKey: Found in patient room{N}{T}in the 2nd floor basement.
ItemDesc_AntiqueShopKey: The tagged key with a shop{N}{T}name on it that Dahlia left{N}{T}at the hospital.
ItemDesc_KeyOfOphiel: The word “Ophiel” is{N}{T}engraved on the key.
ItemDesc_KeyOfHagith: The word “Hagith” is{N}{T}engraved on the key.
ItemDesc_KeyOfPhaleg: The word “Phaleg” is{N}{T}engraved on the key.
ItemDesc_KeyOfBethor: The word “Bethor” is{N}{T}engraved on the key.
ItemDesc_KeyOfAratron: The word “Aratron” is{N}{T}engraved on the key.
ItemDesc_PictureCard: A rectangular card with{N}{T}a picture of a key. Found on{N}{T}a desk in a classroom.
ItemDesc_SewerExitKey: Key to exit from the sewer{N}{T}to the resort area, which was{N}{T}dropped in the water.
ItemDesc_ChannelingStone: A mystical stone possessing{N}{T}power. If used somewhere,{N}{T}the result will be...?
ItemDesc_Chemical: The label reads, “Concentrated{N}{T}hydrochloric acid.” Found in{N}{T}the lab equipment room.
ItemDesc_GoldMedallion: A picture of a clock tower{N}{T}is engraved on the surface.{N}{T}Found in the chemistry lab.
ItemDesc_SilverMedallion: A picture of a clock tower{N}{T}is engraved on the surface.{N}{T}Found in the music room.
ItemDesc_RubberBall: Florescent pink rubber ball{N}{T}used by children.
ItemDesc_Flauros: Pyramid shaped object found{N}{T}in the church.{N}{T}Purpose and use unknown.
ItemDesc_PlasticBottle: Empty bottle found{N}{T}in the kitchen{N}{T}of the hospital.
ItemDesc_UnknownLiquid: Red liquid found in{N}{T}a broken vial in the{N}{T}Hospital Director's room.
ItemDesc_PlateOfTurtle: Square, blue plate{N}{T}with a picture of{N}{T}a “Mock Turtle.”
ItemDesc_PlateOfHatter: Square, green plate{N}{T}with a picture of{N}{T}a “Mad Hatter.”
ItemDesc_PlateOfCat: Square, yellow plate{N}{T}with a picture of{N}{T}a “Cheshire Cat.”
ItemDesc_PlateOfQueen: Square, red plate{N}{T}with a picture of{N}{T}a “Queen of Hearts.”
ItemDesc_BloodPack: 200ml blood pack.{N}{T}Found in the hospital{N}{T}storage room.
ItemDesc_DisinfectingAlcohol: Isopropyl alcohol bottle,{N}{T}found in ICU at the hospital.
ItemDesc_VideoTape: Nothing on the label.{N}{T}Found on 2nd floor{N}{T}hospital basement.
ItemDesc_KaufmannKey: A “3” is written on the tag.{N}{T}The key was thought to have{N}{T}been dropped by Kaufmann.
ItemDesc_Receipt: Receipt from General Store,{N}{T}thought to have been dropped{N}{T}by Kaufmann.
ItemDesc_SafeKey: Key to the safe,{N}{T}found at Indian Runner{N}{T}General Store.
ItemDesc_Magnet: Long, thin magnetic rod{N}{T}with a string attached,{N}{T}in the motel office.
ItemDesc_MotorcycleKey: Key to an old motorcycle,{N}{T}which was dropped between the{N}{T}floorboards in the motel room.
ItemDesc_Pliers: Tool to use on{N}{T}metal and wires.{N}{T}Rusted but usable.
ItemDesc_StoneOfTime: Large stone with clock-face{N}{T}design on pillar with astrology{N}{T}chart hanging from it.
ItemDesc_AmuletOfSolomon: The amulet is a star shaped{N}{T}object with 6 corners named{N}{T}after the ancient magician.
ItemDesc_CrestOfMercury: Snake in the symbol of{N}{T}mercury signifies alchemy.
ItemDesc_DaggerOfMelchior: The ancient dagger{N}{T}named after the Magi.
ItemDesc_DiskOfOuroboros: Ring of a snake biting{N}{T}it's own tail.
ItemDesc_KitchenKnife: Hard to use,{N}{T}but better than nothing.
ItemDesc_SteelPipe: 3' long steel pipe.{N}{T}Long range but of limited use.
ItemDesc_RockDrill: A tool used in road construction{N}{T}to create holes in the road for{N}{T}poles.
ItemDesc_Hammer: Emergency hammer.{N}{T}Highly damaging,{N}{T}but hard to use.
ItemDesc_Chainsaw: Power tool used for cutting{N}{T}down trees, etc., highly deadly.
ItemDesc_Axe: Small one hand axe.{N}{T}Moderately effective{N}{T}as a weapon.
ItemDesc_Handgun: Handgun received from Cybil.{N}{T}Holds up to 15 rounds.
ItemDesc_Shotgun: Fires in a wide radius.{N}{T}Holds up to 6 rounds.
ItemDesc_HyperBlaster: High-powered handgun{N}{T}with infrared scope{N}{T}and unlimited bullets.
ItemDesc_Flashlight: One touch on/off switch.{N}{T}In the chest pocket.
ItemDesc_PocketRadio: Portable radio that{N}{T}emits static{N}{T}when monsters are near.
GameOver_Tip1: When you press the L2 button, the camera{N}angle shifts behind Harry,making it easier{N}to see what is directly in front of him.
GameOver_Tip2: When Harry has beaten an enemy, make{N}sure that he has completely taken it out it out or{N}it may rise again to cause trouble.{N}You will know it is dead when the sound{N}of the radio cuts out.
GameOver_Tip3: Harry can take out an enemy that is lying on the{N}floor by getting out of the weapon ready mode,{N}and then hitting the action button.{N}Harry will kick or step on that enemy to kill it.
GameOver_Tip4: While using a weapon that you swing, the motion{N}will change dependent on how the action button{N}is pressed.{N}Tapping it quickly will make the weapon swing{N}around, while holding it down cause it to swing{N}down orthrust, dependent on the weapon.
GameOver_Tip5: While using guns, hitting the weapon button{N}will aim at the nearest enemy in front of Harry.{N}Htting left or right on the directional button{N}will change the aim on to the next enemy.
GameOver_Tip6: Harry can move around while using{N}some weapons.{N}With this, Harry can back up to avoid{N}attacks by the enemy,while staying{N}in weapon ready mode.
GameOver_Tip7: It is difficult to pin-point flying enemies.{N}However if Harry keep a wall to his back,{N}he can cut down blind-side attacks.
GameOver_Tip8: Harry will be quickly overwhelmed if{N}surrounded by several enemies.{N}When there are manyenemies around,{N}keep your distance or try turning of{N}the light to avoid them.{N}Try to keep confrontations to one-on-one{N}situation.
GameOver_Tip9: It is not always best to confront an enemy{N}on front of Harry. Rather than wasting bullets,{N}sometimes it is better to run.
GameOver_Tip10: Harry is more likely to shoot an enemy{N}when it is close.{N}Draw the enemy into increase accuracy.{N}Harry will save bullets in the long run{N}this way.
GameOver_Tip11: The longer Harry aims at an enemy,{N}the higher the accuracy.{N}Use the search view tobring the view{N}to be right in front of Harry, aim and fire.
GameOver_Tip12: Pushing the L1 and R1 simultaneously{N}will make Harry do a quick turn around.{N}Useful in getting out of a room quickly{N}or running away from enemies.
GameOver_Tip13: When the light is turned off, accuracy{N}with the gun goes way down.{N}When Harry has thelight turned off,{N}carefully approach the enemy and shoot{N}from close range to ensure a hit.
GameOver_Tip14: When Harry has the light turned off, enemies{N}will have a harder time spotting Harry.{N}It willcut down the number of times Harry is{N}surrounded by enemies, lets him avoid battles{N}or even allows Harry to sneak up and attack{N}enemies from behind.
GameOver_Tip15: If you find the night scenes too dark,{N}trying increasing the brightness level{N}in the options menu.
CommonMsg_Someday: Someday, someone may experience{N}these bizarre events. Hopefully,{N}they will find my notes useful.{E}
CommonMsg_NoMap: {D}I don't have the map{N}for this place.{E}
CommonMsg_TooDarkForMap: {D}Too dark to look{N}at the map here.{E}
CommonMsg_TakeFirstAidKit: There is a {C2}First aid kit{C7}.{N}Take it?{S4}
CommonMsg_DoorJammed: The lock is jammed.{N}This door can't be opened.{E}
m0S01Msg_3: {J0(6.0)}{T}Like I've been{N}{T}run over by a truck,{N}{T}but I'm all right, I guess.{E}
m0S01Msg_5: {J0(5.0)}{T}You from around here?{N}{T}Why don't you{N}{T}tell me what happened?
m0S01Msg_7: {J0(5.2)}{T}I came here for a vacation.{N}{T}I just got here...
m0S01Msg_12: {J0(4.0)}{T}Just turned seven last month...{N}{T}Short, black hair...
m0S01Msg_15: {J0(3.0)}{T}The only person I've seen{N}{T}in this town is you.
m0S01Msg_17: {J0(3.5)}{T}I'd tell you if I knew,{N}{T}believe me.
m0S01Msg_18: {J0(5.5)}{T}But, from what I can tell,{N}{T}something bizarre is going on.
m0S01Msg_25: {J0(4.0)}{T}I'm a police officer{N}{T}from Brahms,{N}{T}the next town over.
m0S01Msg_26: {J0(3.0)}{T}The phones are all dead,{N}{T}and the radio, too.
m0S01Msg_27: {J0(4.0)}{T}I'm going back to call{N}{T}in some reinforcements.
m0S01Msg_33: {J0(5.0)}{T}In that case,{N}{T}I need to find her now!{N}{T}Cheryl's my little girl.
m0S01Msg_34: {J0(3.0)}{T}I can't just leave her out{N}{T}there by herself.{E}
m0S01Msg_37: {J0(5.0)}{T}Take this.{N}{T}And hope you don't{N}{T}have to use it.
m0S01Msg_39: {J0(4.5)}{T}before you pull the trigger,{N}{T}know who you're shooting.
m0S01Msg_40: {J0(3.0)}{T}And don't do it{N}{T}unless you have to.{E}
m0S01Msg_44: {J0(5.0)}{T}You'd do best to stay nearby.{N}{T}I'll be back with{N}{T}help as quick as I can.{E}
m0S01Msg_49: {J0(4.5)}{T}Huh? Radio?{N}{T}What's going on with that radio?{E}
m0S01Msg_53: {T}Maybe I should check for{N}{T}something useful before leaving.{E}
m0S01Msg_54: {J0(4.3)}{T}This is not a dream!{N}{T}What's happening to this place!?{E}
m0S01Msg_55: {T}I'll worry about the radio later,{N}{T}I have more important stuff{N}{T}to do.{E}
m0S01Msg_61: {T}I have more important stuff to do.{E}
m0S02Msg_2: {T}Some oil cans are on the shelf.{E}
m0S02Msg_4: {T}There's a TV set.{N}{T}It doesn't turn on.{E}
M1S00Msg_8: {L4}{M}{C3}10:00{N}{T}“Alchemy laboratory”{N}{T}{N}{T}Gold in an old man's palm.{N}{T}The future hidden in his fist.{N}{T}Exchange for sage's water.{E}
M1S00Msg_9: {L4}{M}{C3}12:00{N}{T}“A place with songs and sound”{N}{T}{N}{T}A silver guidepost is{N}{T}untapped in lost tongues.{N}{T}Awakening at the ordained order.{E}
M1S00Msg_10: {L4}{M}{C3}5:00{N}{T}“Darkness that brings{N}{T}the choking heat”{N}{T}{N}{T}Flames render the silence,{N}{T}awakening the hungry beast.{N}{T}Open time's door to beckon prey.{E}
M1S00Msg_12: {T}Cheryl might be here.{N}{T}I should search for her.{E}
M1S00Msg_14: {T}I don't know who drew it,{N}{T}but it is certainly in bad taste.{E}
M1S00Msg_15: {T}There is a switch.{N}{T}Do you want to press the switch?{S4}
M1S00Msg_19: {T}The valve is tightly shut.{N}{T}Can't move it.
M1S00Msg_20: {T}Don't know what it is for,{N}{T}better leave it alone.{E}
M1S00Msg_24: {T}Moore, Ranaldo, Gordon...{N}{T}This must be the list of teachers.{E}
M1S01Msg_2: {T}The fist shut tight,{N}{T}as if never to let go.{E}
M1S01Msg_6: {T}Just the melted remains of{N}{T}a statue of a hand.
M1S01Msg_9: {L4}{M}{C3}“A Tale of Birds Without a Voice”{N}{T}{N}{T}First flew the greedy Pelican,{N}{T}Eager for the reward,{N}{T}White wings flailing.{N}{T}{N}{T}Then came a silent Dove,{N}{T}Flying beyond the Pelican,{N}{T}As far as he could.
M1S01Msg_10: {L4}{M}{C3}A Raven flies in,{N}{T}Flying higher than the Dove,{N}{T}Just to show that he can.{N}{T}{N}{T}A Swan glides in,{N}{T}To find a peaceful spot,{N}{T}Next to another bird.
M1S01Msg_11: {L4}{M}{C3}Finally out comes a Crow,{N}{T}Coming quickly to a stop,{N}{T}Yawning and then napping.{N}{T}{N}{T}Who will show the way,{N}{T}Who will be the key,{N}{T}Who will lead to{N}{T}The silver reward.{E}
M1S01Msg_12: {T}It won't open. Odd,{N}{T}doesn't look locked, though.{E}
M1S01Msg_13: {T}There's some blood{N}{T}on some of the keys.....
M1S01Msg_25: {T}{C3}Warning. Don't forget to{N}{T}take all of your possessions{N}{T} after using the lockers.
M1S01Msg_26: {T}Normal elementary school{N}{T}bulletin board.{N}{T}Nothing in particular here.{E}
M1S01Msg_32: {T}No need to read{N}{T}any of these books.{E}
M1S01Msg_33: {T}There's some blood{N}{T}on some of the keys.....{E}
M1S02Msg_10: {T}If it is, there's no keyhole,{N}{T}or even a knob...
M1S02Msg_11: {T}If I push it, it moves a bit,{N}{T}but it certainly won't open.{E}
M1S02Msg_13: {T}In the center of the door is{N}{T}a horizontal slot.
M1S02Msg_25: {T}The monster lurks...{N}{T}What's that supposed to mean?{E}
M1S02Msg_35: {T}If approached carelessly,{N}{T}I may be sucked in.{E}
M1S02Msg_40: {T}A headless doll is stuck{N}{T}to the wall.{E}
M1S03Msg_4: {C3}{T}...Poltergeists are among these.{N}{T}Negative emotions, like fear,
M1S03Msg_5: {C3}{T}worry or stress manifest{N}{T}into external energy with{N}{T}physical effects.
M1S03Msg_6: {C3}{T}Nightmares have, in some cases,{N}{T}been shown to trigger them.
M1S03Msg_7: {C3}{T}However, such phenomena do not{N}{T}appear to happen to just anyone.
M1S03Msg_8: {C3}{T}Although it's not clear why,{N}{T}adolescents, especially girls,{N}{T}are prone to such occurrences.{E}
M1S03Msg_9: {C3}{T}Hearing this, the hunter{N}{T}armed with bow and arrow said,{N}{T}“I will kill the lizard.”
M1S03Msg_10: {C3}{T}But upon meeting his opponent,{N}{T}he held back, taunting,{N}{T}“Who's afraid of a reptile?”
M1S03Msg_11: {C3}{T}At this, the furious lizard{N}{T}hissed, “I'll swallow you up{N}{T}in a single bite!”
M1S03Msg_12: {C3}{T}Then the huge creature{N}{T}attacked, jaws opened wide.
M1S03Msg_13: {C3}{T}This was what the man wanted.{N}{T}Calmly drawing his bow, he shot{N}{T}into the lizard's gaping mouth.
M1S03Msg_14: {C3}{T}Effortlessly, the arrow flew,{N}{T}piercing the defenseless maw,
M1S03Msg_16: {T}This is from an old fairy tale.{N}{T}I remember reading it as a kid.{E}
M1S03Msg_18: {T}There's a hanging key.{N}{T}It's just out of reach.{E}
M1S03Msg_26: {T}There is a drainage valve.{N}{T}Do you want to open it?{S4}
M1S03Msg_27: {T}It drains into the wrong hole.{N}{T}Maybe I could plug{N}{T}that up somehow.{E}
M1S06Msg_7: {T}The valve is tightly shut.{N}{T}Can't move it.{E}
M1S06Msg_9: {J0(3.0)}{T}A church bell, maybe?{N}{T}Who is ringing the bell?{E}
M1S06Msg_10: {T}Moore, Ranaldo, Gordon...{N}{T}This must be the list of teachers.{E}
M1S06Msg_12: {T}K. Gordon... that's it.{N}{T}I'll note the address down{N}{T}on the map.{E}
M2S00Msg_8: {T}The first picture that Cheryl{N}{T}drew in the sketchbook that I{N}{T}gave on her 5th birthday.{E}
M2S00Msg_11: {T}Probably a doghouse,{N}{T}though I'm not sure{N}{T}since there's no dog around.
M2S00Msg_13: {T}{C2}House key{C7} was hidden{N}{T}in the dog house. Take it?{S4}
M2S00Msg_30: {T}The machinery is running.{N}{T}Do you want to press the switch?{S4}
M2S00Msg_31: {T}The street is blocked.{N}{T}Can't get out of town through here.{E}
M2S00Msg_37: {T}I may be able to break it{N}{T}if I hit it hard.{E}
M2S00Msg_38: {T}Something is written on the map{N}{T}hanging on the wall.
M2S00Msg_40: {T}Something is written on the map{N}{T}hanging on the wall.{E}

M2S00Msg_64: {T}This must be that water works{N}{T}Lisa was talking about.{E}
M2S01Msg_2: {J1(5.3)}{T}I've been expecting you.{N}{T}It was foretold by Gyromancy.
M2S01Msg_11: {J1(4.0)}{T}Nothing is to be gained from{N}{T}floundering about at random.
M2S01Msg_13: {J1(4.0)}{T}The path of the hermit,{N}{T}concealed by Flauros...
M2S01Msg_16: {J0(3.3)}{T}It can break through{N}{T}the walls of darkness,
M2S01Msg_19: {J0(4.2)}{T}Make haste to the hospital{N}{T}before it's too late.{E}
M2S01Msg_25: {T}It must be locked{N}{T}from the other side.{E}
M2S01Msg_28: {T}There's a picture on the wall.{N}{T}Nothing special.{E}
M2S04Msg_3: {C3}{T}Officer Gucci is unlikely to{N}{T}be murdered. He apparently{N}{T}died naturally.
M2S04Msg_4: {C3}{T}But, medical records show{N}{T}Officer Gucci had no prior{N}{T}symptoms of heart disease.{E}
M2S04Msg_6: {C3}{T}Product only available in{N}{T}selected areas of Silent Hill.
M2S04Msg_7: {C3}{T}Raw material is White Claudia,{N}{T}a plant peculiar to the region.
M3S00Msg_5: {J0(3.4)}{T}My name is Harry Mason.{N}{T}I'm in town on vacation.{E}
M3S00Msg_8: {J1(3.6)}{T}I'm Dr. Michael Kaufmann.{N}{T}I work at this hospital.{E}
M3S00Msg_9: {J1(2.5)}{T}So maybe you can tell me{N}{T}what's going on?
M3S00Msg_11: {J1(4.5)}{T}I was taking a nap{N}{T}in the staff room.{N}{T}When I woke up, it was like this.
M3S00Msg_13: {J1(3.0)}{T}And it's snowing out.{N}{T}This time of year.
M3S00Msg_18: {J1(3.5)}{T}You and I both know,{N}{T}creatures like that don't exist!
M3S00Msg_24: {J1(4.0)}{T}But with all those{N}{T}monsters around,{N}{T}I highly doubt that she's...
M3S00Msg_28: {J0(5.0)}{T}She died four years ago.{N}{T}Now, it's just me and my daughter.
M3S00Msg_31: {J0(2.7)}{T}I can't just sit{N}{T}around here doing nothing.
M3S00Msg_35: {T}These are phones and phonebooks.{N}{T}No need to examine this.{E}
M3S01Msg_3: {T}It's not just broken.{N}{T}It looks smashed on purpose.{E}
M3S01Msg_4: {T}The remaining liquid is emptied{N}{T}into the {C2}Plastic bottle{C7}.

M3S01Msg_12: {T}Do you want to press the switch?{S4}
M3S01Msg_16: {T}These are phones and phonebooks.{N}{T}No need to examine this.{E}
M3S01Msg_18: {T}The shelves are all messed up.{N}{T}Who did this?{E}
M3S01Msg_19: {T}Books are scattered{N}{T}around the desk.{N}{T}Nothing else special here.{E}
M3S01Msg_20: {T}The phone's out.{N}{T}Can't get a dial tone.{E}
M3S01Msg_21: {T}There's a white board.{N}{T}Nothing special written here.{E}

M3S03Msg_46: {T}There's a bottle filled{N}{T}with some kind of drug.{N}{T}Better leave it.{E}
M3S03Msg_48: {T}Nothing special in the box.{N}{T}Nothing to worry about.{E}
M3S04Msg_5: {J0(4.7)}{T}Harry, tell me{N}{T}what's happening here?{N}{T}Where is everybody?
M3S04Msg_11: {J1(4.0)}{T}It's like this is{N}{T}all some kind of bad dream.
M3S04Msg_13: {J1(3.6)}{T}Let me ask you, have you seen{N}{T}a little girl around here?
M3S04Msg_18: {J1(4.8)}{T}I can't say that I have.{N}{T}I was unconscious all this time.
M3S04Msg_21: {J1(3.8)}{T}Do you know anything about all{N}{T}that weird stuff in the basement?
M3S04Msg_24: {J1(4.0)}{T}We're under strict orders never to{N}{T}enter the basement storeroom.
M3S04Msg_26: {J1(2.0)}{T}What did you say was down there?

M3S05Msg_9: {T}Can't move it since the vines{N}{T}have grown around it.{E}

M3S05Msg_16: {T}There are skid marks from{N}{T}the cabinet being moved.{E}
M3S05Msg_18: {T}A bed? What's it doing here?{N}{T}Who would...{E}
M3S05Msg_19: {T}A body is laid out on the bed.{N}{T}Don't feel like checking this out.{E}
M3S05Msg_22: {T}There's a bottle filled{N}{T}with some kind of drug.{N}{T}Better leave it.{E}
M3S05Msg_24: {T}Nothing special in the box.{N}{T}Nothing to worry about.{E}

M3S06Msg_10: {J1(4.0)}{T}What are you talking about?{N}{T}I don't understand a word of this.{E}

M4S04Msg_20: {J1(2.9)}{T}Before this place was turned{N}{T}into a resort,
M4S04Msg_21: {J1(3.4)}{T}the townspeople here were{N}{T}on the quiet side.
M4S04Msg_23: {J1(4.8)}{T}Weird occult stuff...{N}{T}Black magic, that kind of thing.
M4S04Msg_24: {J1(5.0)}{T}As young people moved away,{N}{T}the people figured they'd been{N}{T}summoned by the gods.
M4S04Msg_25: {J1(3.7)}{T}Evidently, things like that{N}{T}used to happen around here{N}{T}all the time.
M4S04Msg_26: {J1(3.5)}{T}Before the resort,{N}{T}there really wasn't{N}{T}anything else out here.

M4S04Msg_28: {J1(3.8)}{T}Then a lot of new people{N}{T}came in and everybody{N}{T}clammed up about it.
M4S04Msg_30: {J0(4.8)}{T}Last time I heard anything{N}{T}about it was, gosh, years ago...
M4S04Msg_31: {J1(4.4)}{T}When several people connected{N}{T}with developing the town{N}{T}died in accidents.
M4S04Msg_36: {J0(4.6)}{T}Thank God you came back.{N}{T}I was scared to be here all alone.
M4S04Msg_39: {J1(3.3)}{T}Lisa, can you tell me{N}{T}how to get to the lake?
M4S04Msg_47: {J1(3.3)}{T}There's a water works over by{N}{T}my old elementary school.

M4S04Msg_50: {J1(3.2)}{T}I remember hearing it runs{N}{T}all the way to the lake!{E}
M4S04Msg_52: {J1(2.3)}{T}You think I can get to the lake{N}{T}from there?

M4S04Msg_54: {J1(2.8)}{T}Besides, it's all fenced off{N}{T}to keep people out.
M4S04Msg_55: {J1(2.3)}{T}If there's a chance,{N}{T}I've got to try.{E}
M4S04Msg_57: {J1(4.2)}{T}I don't want to be alone.{N}{T}It's so scary. I can't stand it.

M7S02Msg_11: {L4}{C3}Ask doctor to let me quit being{N}{T}in charge of that patient.{N}{T}{N}{T}It's too weird.{E}
M7S02Msg_12: {L4}{C3}The room is filled with insects.{N}{T}{N}{T}Even with doors{N}{T}and windows shut{N}{T}they get in to spite me.{N}{T}{N}{T}To the hospital...{E}
M7S02Msg_13: {L4}{C3}Still alive,{N}{T}but with wounds that won't heal.{E}
M7S02Msg_14: {L4}{C3}Blood and pus flow from{N}{T}the bathroom faucet.{N}{T}{N}{T}I try to stop it,{N}{T}but it won't turn off.{E}
M7S02Msg_15: {L4}{C3}Feeling bad.{N}{T}Need to throw up.{N}{T}But nothing comes out.{N}{T}Vomiting only bile.{E}
M7S02Msg_17: {L4}{C3}Told the doctor I quit.{N}{T}Won't work at that{N}{T}hospital anymore.{E}
M7S02Msg_25: {J2(3.8)}{T}the blood and pus just start{N}{T}oozing through!
M7S02Msg_33: {J1(6.4)}{T}Everything is going{N}{T}according to plan.{N}{T}Sheltered in the womb.
M7S02Msg_35: {J1(4.5)}{T}Half the soul is lost.{N}{T}That is why the seed{N}{T}lies dormant.
M7S02Msg_36: {J1(3.6)}{T}And what soul remains{N}{T}captured in that husk,
M7S02Msg_38: {J1(2.6)}{T}Are you trying to say{N}{T}it won't work!?
M7S02Msg_41: {J1(4.4)}{T}If we lend a hand,{N}{T}we will be able to get power.
M7S02Msg_42: {J1(5.4)}{T}Never fear.{N}{T}The promise shall not be broken.
M7S02Msg_43: {J1(5.0)}{T}But the power we could{N}{T}draw now would be{N}{T}very weak; almost nothing.
M7S02Msg_44: {J1(3.2)}{T}Unless we get the other{N}{T}half of the soul...
M7S02Msg_46: {J1(6.0)}{T}Feeling this child's pain,{N}{T}it's sure to come.
M7S02Msg_51: {J0(6.3)}{T}I just want you to lend me{N}{T}a teeny bit of your power.{N}{T}That's all.
M7S02Msg_54: {J1(5.8)}{T}It will make everyone happy...{N}{T}and it's for your own good, too.
M7S02Msg_55: {J1(4.3)}{T}Oh, but mommy.{N}{T}I just want to be with you.
M7S02Msg_57: {J1(7.1)}{T}Oh, yes, I see...{N}{T}Maybe mommy has been wrong.
M7S02Msg_59: {J1(5.3)}{T}Why didn't I see this before?{N}{T}There's no reason to wait...
M7S02Msg_60: {J1(7.2)}{T}Herein lies the mother's womb,{N}{T}containing the power{N}{T}to create life.
M7S02Msg_61: {J1(4.2)}{T}I could have done it all myself.
M7S02Msg_70: {T}The generator is working.{N}{T}Do you want to press the switch?{S4}
M7S02Msg_71: {T}The generator is stopped.{N}{T}Do you want to press the switch?{S4}
M7S02Msg_76: {T}Jelly beans.{N}{T}Do you want to open the bag?{S4}
M7S02Msg_88: {C3}Names engraved{N}{T}On a lithograph.{N}{T}The Grim Reaper's list.
M7S02Msg_89: {C3}Yes, the headcount is set{N}{T}Young and old lined up{N}{T}In order of age.
M7S02Msg_90: {C3}Then, the pathway opens{N}{T}Awaiting them, the frenzied{N}{T}Uproar, the feast of death!{E}
M7S02Msg_92: {T}Electricity is flowing to the key.{N}{T}I'll get shocked if I grab it now.{E}
M7S02Msg_96: {T}A steel plate{N}{T}is screwed to the wall.{E}
M7S02Msg_100: {J1(4.0)}{T}Why I'm still alive even{N}{T}though everyone else's dead.
M7S02Msg_101: {J1(4.0)}{T}I'm not the only one{N}{T}who's still walking around.
M7S02Msg_116: {C3}thou possess them{N}{T}to guard thy spirit{N}{T}evoke five rites{N}{T}unveil thy fate{E}
M7S02Msg_117: {T}There's a severed power line.{N}{T}Nothing special here either.{E}
M7S02Msg_121: {T}None of this stuff is of any use.{E}
M7S02Msg_123: {T}There are specimen of{N}{T}butterflies and moths{N}{T}hanging on the wall.{E}
M7S02Msg_125: {T}Six, maybe seven...{N}{T}Must be a kid about that age.{E}
M7S02Msg_128: {T}A child's drawings are scattered{N}{T}on the floor.{E}
M7S02Msg_130: {T}There are old fairy tales,{N}{T}picture books of animals,{N}{T}and plants on the shelf.{E}
M7S02Msg_131: {T}None of this stuff is of any use.{E}
M7S02Msg_133: {T}There is a diary on the floor.{E}
M7S02Msg_135: {T}Looks like an altar.{N}{T}Wonder what they worship?{E}
M7S02Msg_136: {T}The floors and walls are covered{N}{T}with graffiti.{E}
M7S02Msg_138: {T}A body is laid out on the bed.{N}{T}Don't feel like checking this out.{E}
M7S02Msg_139: {T}There's a bottle filled{N}{T}with some kind of drug.{N}{T}Better leave it.{E}
M7S02Msg_141: {T}Nothing special in the box.{N}{T}Nothing to worry about.{E}
M7S02Msg_144: {T}There's a botanical{N}{T}reference book.{N}{T}Don't need to read it now.{E}
M7S03Msg_1: {J1(4.9)}{T}I was shocked to realize{N}{T}the talisman of Metraton{N}{T}was being used.
M7S03Msg_2: {J1(4.0)}{T}In spite of the lost soul{N}{T}returning at last,
M7S03Msg_3: {J1(4.3)}{T}Just a little longer and all{N}{T}would have been for naught.
M7S03Msg_4: {J1(7.0)}{T}It's all because of that man.{N}{T}We must be thankful to him.
M7S03Msg_6: {J1(8.5)}{T}his little girl still has to go.{N}{T}What a pity...{E}
M7S03Msg_11: {J1(5.6)}{T}Well, well, well.{N}{T}To think you'd make it this far.
M7S03Msg_12: {J1(2.5)}{T}Where's Cheryl!?{N}{T}What have you done to her!?
M7S03Msg_14: {J1(4.8)}{T}You've seen her many times.{N}{T}Restored to her former self.
M7S03Msg_18: {J1(4.5)}{T}You are the only one{N}{T}who thinks so.
M7S03Msg_22: {J1(4.4)}{T}For the seven years{N}{T}since that terrible day,
M7S03Msg_25: {J1(6.5)}{T}Alessa has been trapped{N}{T}in an endless nightmare{N}{T}from which she never wakens.
M7S03Msg_30: {J1(5.1)}{T}Everyone will be released,{N}{T}from pain and suffering.
M7S03Msg_33: {J1(4.7)}{T}When all our sorrows{N}{T}will be washed away.
M7S03Msg_35: {J0(6.2)}{T}My daughter will be{N}{T}the mother of God!{E}
M7S03Msg_43: {J1(4.7)}{T}Your role is over.{N}{T}We don't need you anymore.
M7S03Msg_44: {J1(3.6)}{T}What do you think you can{N}{T}accomplish by coming here?
M7S03Msg_46: {J1(3.2)}{T}Bet you can't see this{N}{T}and keep your cool.{E}
M7S03Msg_47: {J0(3.5)}{T}Aglaophotis!{N}{T}I thought I got rid of that!
M7S03Msg_48: {J0(3.2)}{T}All I had to do was plant it{N}{T}somewhere for you to find,
M7S03Msg_65: {J2(4.9)}{T}Descended among us to reach out{N}{T}and save us!{E}
